description: Global site navigation mega menu containing a three-level hierarchical list of top-level sections, sub-sections, and their child links.

props.name: items
props.type: Array<{ label: string; href?: string; children?: Array<{ label: string; href?: string; isUosLink?: boolean; children?: Array<{ label: string; href?: string; isUosLink?: boolean }> }> }>

stories.name: Default
stories.description: Full global navigation with multiple top-level sections and nested sub-menus, mirroring the live site structure.

stories.name: SingleSection
stories.description: Navigation with a single top-level section containing sub-items for isolated testing.

notes: The nav uses a three-level nested ul/li structure. The root ul contains top-level section items (some with no href, acting as labels). Each top-level li may contain a nested ul of sub-section items, each of which may have a further nested ul of leaf links. Some anchor elements carry the class 'uoslink' which is a styled variant. The block is wrapped in Drupal region/block markup (region-header, block-globalnav, block-fixed-block-content, block-fixed-block-contentglobal-nav) which must be preserved verbatim. The nav element has role='navigation' and aria-label='Global menu' — both hardcoded. data-entity-type, data-entity-uuid, data-entity-substitution, and title attributes on anchors are Drupal CMS fields and are ignored as props.
